items.title: Token prices and your AI scribe
items.body: Today's main story is that the 'collapsing AI demand' chart actually shows falling token prices, with one analyst arguing OpenAI could cut prices 60% and stay profitable. Scribe vendors' biggest cost is exactly these tokens. If prices fall, expect either cheaper licences or quietly fatter vendor margins — worth raising at contract renewal.

items.title: The NHS isn't behind — almost everyone is
items.body: The median firm spends $11.38 per employee per month on AI; the dramatic headlines come from the top 1% spending ~$7,500. Useful corrective for any ICB meeting where someone claims the NHS is uniquely slow: most organisations everywhere are barely past the starting line.

items.title: Bezos on workforce — handle with NLW-style scepticism
items.body: Bezos claims AI shrinks teams 10x but creates 10x more opportunity. The same argument is appearing in medical workforce planning debates. The episode's own lesson applies: ask what the data actually measures before accepting the narrative — true for token charts, true for workforce projections.
